query:
---
  WARN-01 resolution — agree, accept it. The SR-05 mechanism is      
  benign log-only code that addresses a High-severity silent         
  signal-loss risk. Relying purely on deployment ordering to prevent 
  the #409 race is operationally fragile. Accept the addition, update
   FR-08 to carve out the conditional warn. The alignment report's
  recommendation is correct.

  ---
  Three things the alignment report missed:
                                           
  1. f32 vs f64 on CO_ACCESS_WEIGHT_UPDATE_DELTA.
  The constant is f32 = 0.1. SQLite REAL fetches as f64. When the    
  tick reads an existing edge weight from GRAPH_EDGES, it gets an f64
   — comparing it against a f32 delta after implicit conversion      
  introduces precision noise. 0.1f32 is actually 0.100000001490116...
   as f64. Should be f64 = 0.1. Minor but the confidence system   
  already burned time on an f32/f64 precision issue in crt-005 —
  worth flagging before delivery rather than after.

  2. AC-13 test doesn't actually discriminate global from local
  normalization.
  The test seeds pairs with counts [1..10], caps at 3, and asserts
  global max = 10 not local max = 8. But because candidates are      
  selected ORDER BY count DESC, the batch always contains the
  highest-count pairs — so global max always equals batch max. The   
  test as written would pass with either normalization strategy. It
  should be reframed as "future-proofing" documentation rather than a
   correctness discriminator, so the delivery agent understands what
  they're actually validating.\
The design intent was always bidirectional via two writes. Pattern 
  #3744 and ADR-006 (#3828) are clear: Direction::Outgoing is the 
  correct enum for the reverse PPR iteration — it IS working as      
  designed. For a symmetric co_access relationship, bidirectionality 
  requires both (A→B) and (B→A) rows in GRAPH_EDGES. PPR traversing  
  Direction::Outgoing from a seeded B will only find A if the A→B row
   exists. The reverse is also needed.                               
                                                                     
  crt-034 v1 writes only (entry_id_a → entry_id_b) — matching the    
  bootstrap. So seeding entry_id_b reaches nothing via co_access.    
  That's the real gap.                                               
                                                                     
  The path is already documented in ADR-006 (#3828): the follow-up   
  must write both (entry_id_a, entry_id_b) and (entry_id_b,          
  entry_id_a) as distinct rows, AND back-fill all bootstrap-era      
  pairs. The UNIQUE(source_id, target_id, relation_type) constraint  
  means the follow-up can safely write reverse edges with no
  collision.

  One additional callout from Pattern #2429: when bidirectional      
  CoAccess edges exist (A→B and B→A), is_cyclic_directed on the full
  graph false-positives as cycles. That's already handled — cycle    
  detection uses a Supersedes-only temp graph. So the follow-up won't
   break cycle detection. The crt-034 spec should reference ADR-006 explicitly in
   the Known Limitations section and confirm the back-fill           
  requirement is part of the follow-up contract. \
Any updates need to be consistent across all design documentation

entries.title: ADR-003 crt-034: Weight Delta as Module-Private f64 Constant
